Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Order And Restraining Order Per Ccp 708.510; Ccp 708.520
Off Calendar Mar-14-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. GREG GROENENVELD, Esq. of Law Offices of Greg Groeneveld (tel: 415-308-3637) for Plaintiffs.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF BAUER'S INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.'s Motion For Assignment Order And Restraining Order Per Ccp 708.510; Ccp 708.520.

Matter discussed. Counsel Greg Groenenveld requests additional languange in the order. Upon conclusion, the Court grants counsel's request and adopts its tentative ruling with modifications:

The motion of judgment creditor Bauer's Intelligent Transportation Inc. for an assignment order is granted pursuant to CCP 708.510. No filed opposition appears in the court's register of actions.

The court orders that the following rights to payment of Judgment Debtor, Fadi Stephan, be, and hereby are, assigned to the judgment creditor, Bauer's Intelligent Transportation, Inc., until such time as the judgment herein is fully satisfied or this order is amended: all rights to rental payments due, or which may become due and payable, from the real property located at 2381 Alta Garden Ln, Unit 58, Sacramento, CA 95825.

The court further orders judgment debtor Fadi Stephan to instruct tenants paying rent for the real property located at 2381 Alta Garden Ln, Unit 58, Sacramento, CA 95825 to make rental payments to the judgment creditor's attorney (Greg Groeneveld, Suite 600, 27 Maiden Ln., San Francisco, CA 94108) for the benefit of the judgment creditor, Bauer's Intelligent Transportation, Inc.

The court further orders Fadi Stephan not to transfer, assign, spend, give away or otherwise dispose of his interests in rental payments due, or which may become due, from the real property located at 2381 Alta Garden Ln, Unit 58, Sacramento, CA 95825. In the event Fadi Stephan receives such rental payments, he is ordered to forward those payments to the judgment creditor's attorney (Greg Groeneveld, Suite 600, 27 Maiden Ln., San Francisco, CA 94108) for the benefit of the judgment creditor, Bauer's Intelligent Transportation, Inc., and he must endorse those payments so that they may be deposited in the trust account of judgment creditor's attorney.

Judgment creditor is ordered to file a partial satisfaction of judgment no less frequently than quarterly reflecting the receipt of any rental payments assigned by this Order.

In the event judgment creditor's attorney retires or otherwise ceases to represent judgment creditor, the attorney who takes over representation of judgment creditor shall notify Fadi Stephan, provide him with a copy of the file endorsed substitution of attorney and provide Fadi Stephan with their mailing address and, thereafter, any orders directing payment to Greg Groeneveld, Esq. contained herein shall require payment to that new attorney.

Judgment creditor shall personally serve this order on judgment debtor Fadi Stephan. Fadi Stephan is notified that if he fails to comply with this order he may be subject to arrest and punishment for contempt of court.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day, having been continued from September 16, 2021 for further Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Fadi Joseph Stephan, an individual.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:14 a.m.

Nicholas A. Carlin, Esq. and Robert Carroll, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Bauer's Intelligent Transporation, Inc., a California corporation.
Gary Bauer, Founder and CEO of Bauer's Intelligent Transporation, Inc. was placed under oath, but did not testify.
No appearance by defendant Fadi Joseph Stephan, an individual.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Damages was filed on February 5, 2016.
Default as to defendant Fadi Joseph Stephan, an individual, was entered on March 21, 2016.
Order setting aside the default as to Mr. Stephan was filed Mary 13, 20165
Plaintiff First Amended Complaint for Damages was filed on September 14, 2016, and served on defendant Fadi Joseph Stephan, an individual, on October 11, 2016.
Default as to defendant Fadi Joseph Stephan, an individual, on plaintiff's First Amended Complaint was entered on November 28, 2016.

At the last calling of the case, the Court admitted into evidence Exhibits A through I attached to Declarations of Gary Bauer, filed with the Clerk's Office on July 29, 2021 & July 30, 2021, and the exhibits attached to the Declarations of Custodian of Records, James Rich.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing therefor, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Bauer's Intelligent Transporation, Inc., and against the defaulted defendant Fadi Joseph Stephan, an individual, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $4,221,378.29.
Prejudgment Interest - $2,266,822.31.
Attorney Fees - $273,592.00.
Costs - $10,627.25.

Total - $6,772,419.85.

Additionally, plaintiff Bauer's Intelligent Transportation, Inc.'s Motion to Seal exhibits D through I of the Bauer Declaration is GRANTED.

Lastly, plaintiff Bauer's Intelligent Transportaion, Inc.'s ("plaintiff") request for an INJUNCTION against defendant Fadi Joseph Stephan, an individual, is GRANTED as follows:
(1) Defendant Stephen shall not access plaintiff's COMPUTER SYSTEMS and/or PROTECTED INFORMATION in any manner for any purpose;
(2) Defendant Stephan shall not use plaintiff's PROTECTED INFORMATION, including to solicit or receive business from plaintiff's clients and client representatives;
(3) Defendant Stephan shall not actually misappropriate or threaten to misappropriate plaintiff's PROTECTED INFORMATION as prohibited by California's Uniform Trade Secrets Act and the United States of America's Defend Trade Secrets Act;
(4) Defendant Stephan shall not falsify or cause others to falsify plaintiff's business records;
(5) Defendant Stephan shall not submit false and fraudulent invoices to plaintiff's for payment. Defendant Stephan shall not make further demands for payment of money from plaintiff unless and until plaintiff is made whole from the losses caused by Defendants as alleged herein; and
(6) Defendant Stephen shall immediately stop using the telephone number 415-740-5210 and shall take all steps necessary to return full control, ownership, and use of that phone number to plaintiff, including but not limited to directing his telecommunications carrier(s) to transfer the number to plaintiff. The Court directs any party acting in concert with, as an agent of, aiding and/or abetting defendant Stephan in maintaining control of this telephone number, including any telecommunications carriers, to return full control, ownership, and use of that phone number to plaintiff.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Proceedings concluded at 10:52 a.m.

Defendant Professional Charter Services' motion to stay the case pending the resolution of the criminal case against defendant Fadi Stephan is continued to Janaury 12, 2018 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 (Friday) for a status report on Mr. Stephan's trial date and to decide whether the August 6, 2018 trial date should remain in effect, be continued or vacated. MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-16-550762 WITH CASE CGC-16-550293
Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Corie A. Edwards of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP (415-398-0200) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party, Bauer's Intelligent Transportation

No other appearance is made in this action.

The cause is argued concurrently with related action CGC-16-550762, Social Trends, Inc. vs. Bauer's Intelligent Transportation.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF BAUER'S INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-16-550762 WITH CASE CGC-16-550293 and for leave to filed and amended and unified complaint is granted in its entirety. This case and Social Trends, Inc. v. Bauer's Intelligent Transportation, Inc. et al CGC-550762 are consolidated for all purposes subject to an order of the trial judge bifurcating any issues for purposes of the trial. The similarity of the issues, facts, and parties warrants consolidating and doing so promotes judicial economy. This case is the lead case and all further filings for either case will be in this case only. Bauer's is given leave to file an amended and unified complaint in these two cases. Granting such leave is consistent with the strong policy favoring the allowance of amendments to pleadings, will promote judicial economy, and will not prejudice any party. So that there is no confusion the unified complaint should be entitled something like "Unified Complaint Constituting the First Amended Complaint in Case 550293 and the Second Amended Cross-Complaint in Case 550762" but the body of the complaint may refer to Bauer's as the plaintiff and all parties sued therein as defendants. Doing this retains Social Trends' status as the plaintiff in case 550762. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)
